dimension: Permitting / approval lead time (regulatory clock to bring a firm-power option online to serve a data center, NOT physical construction)
metric: Years of permitting, licensing and regulatory approval required before the option can begin serving the data center (permitting/approval clock only). Lower years = more deployable.
normalization: score = clamp(1 - permitting_years / 8, 0, 1), evaluated at the midpoint of each option's typical permitting/approval clock in years; shorter permitting = higher score. The 8-year denominator anchors the worst realistic permitting horizon (geothermal up-to-10-yr / historical nuclear decade) toward 0; a zero-year (already-permitted) option approaches 1.

scores.metric_value: ~0.5 years (already FERC-licensed; needs a PPA, not a new generation permit)
scores.rationale: Plant already holds a FERC license, so firming a data center needs only a power-purchase agreement / minor amendment, not a new generation permit -- effectively no new approval clock.

sources.name: CRS R48762 -- Data Center Energy Infrastructure: Federal Permit Requirements (existing hydro PPAs, e.g. Brookfield-Google)

scores.rationale: New-build air permit plus state siting; streamlined CEC opt-in path is ~270-360 days, with simple permits ~6-12 months and complex EIS cases stretching toward ~2 years.

sources.name: California Energy Commission -- Power Plant Licensing (270-360 day opt-in window)
US EPA -- Clean Air Act power plant permitting

scores.metric_value: ~3-4 years (siting permits + interconnection queue; ~5-yr median queue in 2023)
scores.rationale: New-build siting is months-to-~1 yr, but the binding regulatory/approval bottleneck is the interconnection queue: LBNL reports a ~5-year median request-to-operation in 2023 (up from <2 yr in 2008).

sources.name: LBNL 'Queued Up' (2024 ed.) -- ~5-yr median queue duration, 2023
Utility Dive -- LBNL queue data (2 yr in 2008 -> 5 yr in 2023)

scores.technology: Solar plus wind with long-duration storage
scores.metric_value: ~3-4.5 years (same host siting + queue, plus newer LDES/BESS siting friction)
scores.rationale: Inherits the same interconnection-queue bottleneck as its solar+wind host, plus added storage-siting friction (local BESS ordinances/moratoria, EU proposals for 12-24 mo limits), nudging it slightly above the 4-hr case in permitting time.

sources.name: LBNL 'Queued Up' (2024 ed.) -- ~5-yr median queue duration
Utility Dive -- LBNL interconnection queue wait times

scores.metric_value: ~3-5+ years (NRC combined license; ADVANCE Act targets ~25 mo post-docketing, plus pre-application; historically ~a decade)
scores.rationale: NRC combined-license/design review: ADVANCE Act mandates safety+environmental review in 18 mo and a final decision within 25 mo of docketing, but pre-application and design certification push the realistic approval clock to ~3-5+ yr (historically >10 yr).

sources.name: NRC -- ADVANCE Act Licensing Efficiencies (18-mo review, 25-mo final decision)
NRC -- Combined License Applications for New Reactors

scores.metric_value: ~5-7 years permitting (up to 6 separate NEPA reviews; 7-10 yr total development)
scores.rationale: Longest regulatory clock: DOE notes a single site can trigger NEPA up to six separate times (planning, leasing, exploration, drilling, utilization); GeoVision finds permitting adds 5-7 yr and total development reaches 7-10 yr.

sources.name: DOE -- Permitting for Geothermal Power Development Projects (NEPA up to six times)
DOE OSTI -- Geothermal Permitting and NEPA Timeline Analysis
